chapter_title.ar: بَابُ صَوْمِ الْمُتَمَتِّعِ إِذَا لَمْ يَجِدِ الْهَدْيَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 191 – The Fasting of the performer of Tumatto when he cannot find (afford) the sacrificial animal

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/198)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/198)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ مُتَمَتِّعٍ صَامَ ثَلَاثَةَ أَيَّامٍ فِي الْحَجِّ ثُمَّ أَصَابَ هَدْياً يَوْمَ خَرَجَ مِنْ مِنًى قَالَ أَجْزَأَهُ صِيَامُهُ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Husayn Bin Saeed, from Abdullah Bin Bahr, from Hammad Bin Usman who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullahasws about the performer of Tumatto Fasting for three days during the Hajj, then he comes across a sacrificial animal on the day he exits from Makkah. Heasws said: ‘His Fasts would suffice him’.
en.sarwar: 11. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Husayn ibn Sa‘id from ‘Abd Allah ibn Bahr from Hammad ibn ‘Uthaman who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man for al-Tamattu‘ (advantageous) who fasts for three days and on the day of departure finds an offering animal. He (the Imam) said, ‘His fast is sufficient.’”
